name: ntptime
description: NTP (Network Time Protocol) client for synchronizing the ESP32 RTC with internet time servers. Built into MicroPython firmware.
differences_from_cpython: This module does NOT exist in CPython. It is MicroPython-specific for embedded time synchronization.

functions.name: ntptime.settime
functions.description: Query the configured NTP server and set the ESP32 RTC to the received time. Blocks during network request (~1-2 seconds).
functions.note: Must have Wi-Fi connected BEFORE calling this. Raises OSError/ETIMEDOUT on failure.

functions.name: ntptime.time

returns.description: (year, month, day, hour, minute, second, weekday, yearday). Weekday 0-6 (Mon=0).

functions.description: Query NTP server and return time as tuple. Does NOT set the RTC.
functions.note: Returns time in UTC. MicroPython epoch is 2000-01-01.

properties.name: ntptime.host
properties.description: NTP server hostname. Default: 'pool.ntp.org'. Set before calling settime().

example:
import network
import ntptime
import time

# Connect Wi-Fi first
wlan = network.WLAN(network.STA_IF)
wlan.active(True)
wlan.connect('MyWiFi', 'password')
while not wlan.isconnected():
    time.sleep_ms(100)

# Optional: use a closer NTP server
ntptime.host = 'ntp.aliyun.com'

# Sync RTC
ntptime.settime()
print('Time synced:', time.localtime())
esp32_notes: ESP32 RTC maintains time during deep sleep (if RTC domain is powered).
After deep sleep wake, call ntptime.settime() again if network reconnects.
The 2000-01-01 epoch means time.time() returns seconds since 2000, NOT 1970.
For periodic sync, call settime() every 1-24 hours (NTP drift on ESP32 is ~1-2s/day).
common_bugs: Calling ntptime.settime() without Wi-Fi connected — raises OSError.
Forgetting to call wlan.active(True) and wlan.connect() first.
Assuming time.time() returns Unix timestamp (1970 epoch) — it uses 2000 epoch on ESP32.
Not waiting for Wi-Fi to fully connect (wlan.isconnected() == True) before calling settime().
internal_imports_note: Module imports time.gmtime, socket, struct for internal use. These may appear in dir(ntptime) but are not part of the public API.
